title: Probiotics and COVID-19
author: Domina Petric
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: SARS-CoV-2 is an enveloped virus with a single-stranded positive sense RNA genome. Vaccine efficacy can be limited by mutations in RNA viruses and search for alternative/additional effective therapies must continue. Probiotics are generally considered safe. There is a good scientific evidence to support the ability of probiotics to boost human immunity. It is rationale to support the use of probiotics in prevention and treatment of viral infections, including COVID-19. In addition to that, the use of probiotics in COVID-19 can serve as protective factor against cytokine storm and consequential autoimmunity.
